title: thelypteris quadrangularis var. inconstans
intro: Thelypteris quadrangularis var. inconstans, also known as the Inconstant Maidenhair Fern, is a variety of the Thelypteris quadrangularis species, a type of fern native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The Inconstant Maidenhair Fern has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and easing digestive problems, due to its bioactive compounds and antioxidants. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the Inconstant Maidenhair Fern are typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or as a component in tinctures and ointments.
